records.chapter: Reward of repenting
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.translator: Sayed Athar Husain Rizvi & Sayed Maqsood Athar
records.englishText: 5.  Imam  Muhammad  (a.s.)  narrates  that  the  Holy  Prophet (s.a.w.s.) said, “One who has Astaghfirullaah written under each  of his sin on the Day of Judgment is indeed fortunate.”
records.arabicText: أَبِی ره عَنْ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ بْنِ جَعْفَرٍ عَنْ هَارُونَ بْنِ مُسْلِمٍ عَنْ مَسْعَدَهَ بْنِ صَدَقَهَ عَنْ جَعْفَرٍ الصَّادِقِ عَنْ أَبِیهِ عَنْ آبَائِهِ ع قَالَ قَالَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ص طُوبَی لِمَنْ وَجَدَ فِی صَحِیفَتِهِ یَوْمَ الْقِیَامَهِ تَحْتَ کُلِّ ذَنْبٍ أَسْتَغْفِرُ اللَّهَ.
records.thaqalaynSanad: 5.  Imam  Muhammad  (a.s.)  narrates  that  the  Holy  Prophet (s.a.w.s.) said,
records.thaqalaynMatn: “One who has Astaghfirullaah written under each  of his sin on the Day of Judgment is indeed fortunate.”

records.chapter: Reward of repenting seventy times every day in the month of Shaban
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.translator: Sayed Athar Husain Rizvi & Sayed Maqsood Athar
records.englishText: 1. My father (rah) said: it was narrated to me by Sa’d b. Abdillah who said it was narrated to me by Musa b. Ja’far al-Baghdadi from Muhammad b. Jumhur from Abdillah b. Abdulrahman from Muhammad b. Abi Hamzah from Abi Abdillah (a.s.) who said: “One who recites Astaghfirullaahalladhee laa ilaaha illaa huwar rah’maanur rah’eemul h’ayyul qayyoomu wa atoobu ilaih seventy times every day in the month of Shaban his name will be noted in Afaq-e-Mubeen.” The narrator asked, “What is this Afaq-e- Mubeen?” There is a vast forest in front of the Divine Throne, which has springs flowing in it and has cups equal to the number of stars.”
records.arabicText: أَبِی ره قَالَ حَدَّثَنِی سَعْدُ بْنُ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ قَالَ حَدَّثَنِی مُوسَی بْنُ جَعْفَرٍ الْبَغْدَادِیُّ عَنْ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ جُمْهُورٍ عَنْ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ بْنِ عَبْدِ الرَّحْمَنِ عَنْ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ أَبِی حَمْزَهَ عَنْ أَبِی عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ع قَالَ: مَنْ قَالَ فِی کُلِّ یَوْمٍ مِنْ شَعْبَانَ سَبْعِینَ مَرَّهً أَسْتَغْفِرُ اللَّهَ الَّذِی لَا إِلَهَ إِلَّا هُوَ الرَّحْمَنُ الرَّحِیمُ الْحَیُّ الْقَیُّومُ وَ أَتُوبُ إِلَیْهِ کُتِبَ فِی الْأُفُقِ الْمُبِینِ قُلْتُ وَ مَا الْأُفُقُ الْمُبِینُ قَالَ قَاعٌ بَیْنَ یَدَیِ الْعَرْشِ فِیهِ أَنْهَارٌ تَطَّرِدُ فِیهِ الْقِدْحَانُ عَدَدَ النُّجُومِ.
records.thaqalaynSanad: 1. My father (rah) said: it was narrated to me by Sa’d b. Abdillah who said it was narrated to me by Musa b. Ja’far al-Baghdadi from Muhammad b. Jumhur from Abdillah b. Abdulrahman from Muhammad b. Abi Hamzah from Abi Abdillah (a.s.) who said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: “One who recites Astaghfirullaahalladhee laa ilaaha illaa huwar rah’maanur rah’eemul h’ayyul qayyoomu wa atoobu ilaih seventy times every day in the month of Shaban his name will be noted in Afaq-e-Mubeen.” The narrator asked, “What is this Afaq-e- Mubeen?” There is a vast forest in front of the Divine Throne, which has springs flowing in it and has cups equal to the number of stars.”

records.chapter: Reward of repenting seventy times after Morning Prayer
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.translator: Sayed Athar Husain Rizvi & Sayed Maqsood Athar
records.englishText: 1. It was narrated to me by Muhammad b. Ali Majluwayh from Muhammad b. Ahmad from Ali b. al-Sindi from Muhammad b. ’Amru b. Sahl from Harun b. Kharija from Jabir al-Ju’fi from Abi Ja’far (a.s.) who said: “One who seeks forgiveness of Allah seventy times after the Morning Prayer Allah forgives him even if he has committed seventy thousand sins in the previous day and one who has committed more than seventy thousand sins there is no good for him.”
records.arabicText: حَدَّثَنِی مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ عَلِیٍّ مَاجِیلَوَیْهِ عَنْ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ أَحْمَدَ عَنْ عَلِیِّ بْنِ السِّنْدِیِّ عَنْ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ عَمْرِو بْنِ سَهْلٍ عَنْ هَارُونَ بْنِ خَارِجَهَ عَنْ جَابِرٍ الْجُعْفِیِّ عَنْ أَبِی جَعْفَرٍ ع قَالَ: مَنِ اسْتَغْفَرَ اللَّهَ بَعْدَ صَلَاهِ الْفَجْرِ سَبْعِینَ مَرَّهً غَفَرَ اللَّهُ لَهُ وَ لَوْ عَمِلَ ذَلِکَ الْیَوْمَ أَکْثَرَ مِنْ سَبْعِینَ أَلْفَ ذَنْبٍ وَ مَنْ عَمِلَ أَکْثَرَ مِنْ سَبْعِینَ أَلْفَ ذَنْبٍ فَلَا خَیْرَ فِیهِ.
records.thaqalaynSanad: 1. It was narrated to me by Muhammad b. Ali Majluwayh from Muhammad b. Ahmad from Ali b. al-Sindi from Muhammad b. ’Amru b. Sahl from Harun b. Kharija from Jabir al-Ju’fi from Abi Ja’far (a.s.) who said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: “One who seeks forgiveness of Allah seventy times after the Morning Prayer Allah forgives him even if he has committed seventy thousand sins in the previous day and one who has committed more than seventy thousand sins there is no good for him.”

records.chapter: Reward of testifying the Oneness of Allah and prophethood of Muhammad (s.a.w.s.), saying Innaa lillaahi wa innaa ilaihi raaji-oo’n at the time of difficulty, saying Alh’amdulillaah on hearing a good news and saying Astaghfirullaah on committing a sin
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.translator: Sayed Athar Husain Rizvi & Sayed Maqsood Athar
records.englishText: 1.  Imam Ja’far Sadiq (a.s.) narrates from his predecessors that the  Holy  Prophet  (s.a.w.s.)  said,  “One  who  performs  four deeds, becomes deserving of a great light of Allah. They are: testifying that there is no Lord except Allah and I am His prophet, saying Innaa  lillaahi  wa  innaa  ilaihi  raaji-oo’n  at the time of difficulty, saying Alh’amdulillaah  on hearing a good news and saying Astaghfirullaah on committing a sin.”
records.arabicText: أَبِی ره قَالَ حَدَّثَنِی عَلِیُّ بْنُ مُوسَی عَنْ أَحْمَدَ بْنِ مُحَمَّدٍ عَنْ بَکْرِ بْنِ صَالِحٍ عَنِ الْحَسَنِ بْنِ عَلِیٍّ عَنْ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ بْنِ عَلِیٍّ عَنْ عَلِیِّ بْنِ عَلِیٍّ اللَّهَبِیِّ عَنْ جَعْفَرٍ الصَّادِقِ عَنْ أَبِیهِ عَنْ آبَائِهِ ع قَالَ قَالَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ص أَرْبَعٌ مَنْ کُنَّ فِیهِ کَانَ فِی نُورِ اللَّهِ الْأَعْظَمِ مَنْ کَانَ فِی عِصْمَهِ أَمْرِهِ شَهَادَهُ أَنْ لَا إِلَهَ إِلَّا اللَّهُ وَ أَنِّی رَسُولُ اللَّهِ وَ مَنْ إِذَا أَصَابَتْهُ مُصِیبَهٌ قَالَ إِنَّا لِلَّهِ وَ إِنَّا إِلَیْهِ راجِعُونَ وَ مَنْ إِذَا أَصَابَ خَیْراً قَالَ الْحَمْدُ لِلَّهِ وَ مَنْ إِذَا أَصَابَ خَطِیئَهً قَالَ أَسْتَغْفِرُ اللَّهَ وَ أَتُوبُ إِلَیْهِ.

records.chapter: Deeds which are rewarded soon
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.translator: Sayed Athar Husain Rizvi & Sayed Maqsood Athar
records.englishText: 1.  Imam Ja’far Sadiq (a.s.) narrates from his predecessors that the Holy Prophet (s.a.w.s.) said, “The deed that is rewarded soon  is  a  good  action  and  the  deed  punished  soon  is injustice. The deed is bad enough when a person sees faults in others but is blind as far as his self is concerned and he advises  people  to  avoid  sins  but  does  not  avoid  them himself  and  if  he  troubles  with  useless  talk  those  who  sit with him.”
records.arabicText: أَبِی ره قَالَ حَدَّثَنِی عَلِیُّ بْنُ مُوسَی عَنْ أَحْمَدَ بْنِ مُحَمَّدٍ عَنْ بَکْرِ بْنِ صَالِحٍ عَنِ الْحَسَنِ بْنِ عَلِیِّ بْنِ فَضَّالٍ عَنْ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ بْنِ إِبْرَاهِیمَ عَنِ الْحَسَنِ بْنِ یَزِیدَ عَنْ جَعْفَرٍ عَنْ أَبِیهِ عَنْ آبَائِهِ ع قَالَ قَالَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ص إِنَّ أَسْرَعَ الْخَیْرِ ثَوَاباً الْبِرُّ وَ إِنَّ أَسْرَعَ الشَّرِّ عِقَاباً الْبَغْیُ وَ کَفَی بِالْمَرْءِ عَیْباً أَنْ یَنْظُرَ مِنَ النَّاسِ إِلَی مَا یَعْمَی عَنْهُ مِنْ نَفْسِهِ أَوْ یُعَیِّرَ النَّاسَ مَا لَا یَسْتَطِیعُ تَرْکَهُ أَوْ یُؤْذِیَ جَلِیسَهُ بِمَا لَا یَعْنِیهِ.
records.thaqalaynSanad: 1.  Imam Ja’far Sadiq (a.s.) narrates from his predecessors that the Holy Prophet (s.a.w.s.) said,
records.thaqalaynMatn: “The deed that is rewarded soon  is  a  good  action  and  the  deed  punished  soon  is injustice. The deed is bad enough when a person sees faults in others but is blind as far as his self is concerned and he advises  people  to  avoid  sins  but  does  not  avoid  them himself  and  if  he  troubles  with  useless  talk  those  who  sit with him.”

records.chapter: Reward of reciting Sub-h’aanallaah thrice in the morning and evening
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.translator: Sayed Athar Husain Rizvi & Sayed Maqsood Athar
records.englishText: 1. My father (rah) said: it was narrated to me by Ali b. Musa from Ahmad b. Muhammad from Ali b. al-Hakam from Sayf from Abdulrahman b. Sayabeh from Ibn Ishaq from al-Harith from Ameer al-Mumineen (a.s.) who said: “One who recites Fa sub- h’aanallaahi h’eena tumsoona wa h’eena tus’bih’oona wa lahul h’amdu fis samaawaati wal arz”i wa a’shiyyanw wa h’eena tuz’hiroon thrice at night has received all the rewards of that night and distanced all the mischief. One who recites it in the morning will receive all the good and kept away from all the mischief of that day.”
records.arabicText: أَبِی ره قَالَ حَدَّثَنِی عَلِیُّ بْنُ مُوسَی عَنْ أَحْمَدَ بْنِ مُحَمَّدٍ عَنْ عَلِیِّ بْنِ الْحَکَمِ عَنْ سَیْفٍ عَنْ عَبْدِ الرَّحْمَنِ بْنِ سَیَابَهَ عَنِ ابْنِ إِسْحَاقَ عَنِ الْحَارِثِ عَنْ أَمِیرِ الْمُؤْمِنِینَ ع قَالَ: مَنْ قَالَ حِینَ یُمْسِی ثَلَاثَ مَرَّاتٍ- فَسُبْحانَ اللَّهِ حِینَ تُمْسُونَ وَ حِینَ تُصْبِحُونَ. وَ لَهُ الْحَمْدُ فِی السَّماواتِ وَ الْأَرْضِ وَ عَشِیًّا وَ حِینَ تُظْهِرُونَ لَمْ یَفُتْهُ خَیْرٌ یَکُونُ فِی تِلْکَ اللَّیْلَهِ وَ صُرِفَ عَنْهُ جَمِیعُ شَرِّهَا وَ مَنْ قَالَ مِثْلَ ذَلِکَ حِینَ یُصْبِحُ لَمْ یَفُتْهُ خَیْرٌ یَکُونُ فِی ذَلِکَ الْیَوْمِ وَ صُرِفَ عَنْهُ جَمِیعُ شَرِّهِ.
records.thaqalaynSanad: 1. My father (rah) said: it was narrated to me by Ali b. Musa from Ahmad b. Muhammad from Ali b. al-Hakam from Sayf from Abdulrahman b. Sayabeh from Ibn Ishaq from al-Harith from Ameer al-Mumineen (a.s.) who said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: “One who recites Fa sub- h’aanallaahi h’eena tumsoona wa h’eena tus’bih’oona wa lahul h’amdu fis samaawaati wal arz”i wa a’shiyyanw wa h’eena tuz’hiroon thrice at night has received all the rewards of that night and distanced all the mischief. One who recites it in the morning will receive all the good and kept away from all the mischief of that day.”

records.chapter: Reward of abstinence and piety
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.translator: Sayed Athar Husain Rizvi & Sayed Maqsood Athar
records.englishText: 1. It was narrated to me by Muhammad b. al-Hassan who said it was narrated to me by Muhammad b. al-Hassan al-Saffar from al-Abbas b. Ma’ruf from Ali b. Mahziyar from Ja’far b. Basheer from Sayf from Abi Abdillah (a.s.) who said: “One who does not feel shy in earning his sustenance and meets his expenses is prosperous and has a good family. One who observes piety in the world, Allah fills his heart with wisdom, makes his tongue speak, keeps him away from worldly defects, diseases and their treatments and takes him to the ‘abode of peace’ (Daarus Salaam) safe and sound.”
records.arabicText: حَدَّثَنِی مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ الْحَسَنِ قَالَ حَدَّثَنِی مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ الْحَسَنِ الصَّفَّارُ عَنِ الْعَبَّاسِ بْنِ مَعْرُوفٍ عَنْ عَلِیِّ بْنِ مَهْزِیَارَ عَنْ جَعْفَرِ بْنِ بَشِیرٍ عَنْ سَیْفٍ عَنْ أَبِی عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ع قَالَ: مَنْ لَمْ یَسْتَحْیِ مِنْ طَلَبِ الْمَعَاشِ خَفَّتْ مَئُونَتُهُ وَ رَخِیَ بَالُهُ وَ نُعِّمَ عِیَالُهُ وَ مَنْ زَهِدَ فِی الدُّنْیَا أَنْبَتَ اللَّهُ الْحِکْمَهَ فِی قَلْبِهِ وَ انْطَلَقَ بِهَا لِسَانُهُ وَ بَصَّرَهُ عُیُوبَ الدُّنْیَا دَاءَهَا وَ دَوَاءَهَا وَ أَخْرَجَهُ مِنْهَا سَالِماً إِلَی دَارِ السَّلَامِ.
records.thaqalaynSanad: 1. It was narrated to me by Muhammad b. al-Hassan who said it was narrated to me by Muhammad b. al-Hassan al-Saffar from al-Abbas b. Ma’ruf from Ali b. Mahziyar from Ja’far b. Basheer from Sayf from Abi Abdillah (a.s.) who said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: “One who does not feel shy in earning his sustenance and meets his expenses is prosperous and has a good family. One who observes piety in the world, Allah fills his heart with wisdom, makes his tongue speak, keeps him away from worldly defects, diseases and their treatments and takes him to the ‘abode of peace’ (Daarus Salaam) safe and sound.”

records.chapter: Reward of performing a good deed at the start and end of the day
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.translator: Sayed Athar Husain Rizvi & Sayed Maqsood Athar
records.englishText: 1.  Imam  Muhammad  Baqir  (a.s.)  narrates  that  the  Holy Prophet (s.a.w.s.) said, “Surely an angel descends carrying a scroll in the beginning of day and night and writes the deeds of men. Hence one should do good deeds in the beginning of day and night. God willing, you will be forgiven during the other times also if Allah wills, because Allah, the Mighty and   Sublime   says:   ‘Therefore   remember   Me,   I   will remember   you’. And   He   says:   ‘and   certainly   the remembrance of Allah is the greatest’.”
records.arabicText: أَبِی ره قَالَ حَدَّثَنِی عَبْدُ اللَّهِ بْنُ جَعْفَرٍ الْحِمْیَرِیُّ عَنْ إِبْرَاهِیمَ بْنِ مَهْزِیَارَ عَنْ أَخِیهِ عَلِیِّ بْنِ مَهْزِیَارَ عَنْ عَمْرِو بْنِ شِمْرٍ عَنِ الْفَضْلِ عَنْ جَابِرٍ عَنْ أَبِی جَعْفَرٍ ع قَالَ قَالَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ص إِنَّ الْمَلَکَ یَنْزِلُ بِصَحِیفَهٍ أَوَّلَ النَّهَارِ وَ أَوَّلَ اللَّیْلِ فَیَکْتُبُ فِیهَا عَمَلَ ابْنِ آدَمَ فَاعْمَلُوا فِی أَوَّلِهَا خَیْراً وَ فِی آخِرِهَا خَیْراً فَإِنَّ اللَّهَ یَغْفِرُ لَکُمْ فِیمَا بَیْنَ ذَلِکَ إِنْ شَاءَ اللَّهُ فَإِنَّ اللَّهَ عَزَّ وَ جَلَّ یَقُولُ فَاذْکُرُونِی أَذْکُرْکُمْ وَ یَقُولُ وَ لَذِکْرُ اللَّهِ أَکْبَرُ
records.thaqalaynSanad: 1.  Imam  Muhammad  Baqir  (a.s.)  narrates  that  the  Holy Prophet (s.a.w.s.) said,
records.thaqalaynMatn: “Surely an angel descends carrying a scroll in the beginning of day and night and writes the deeds of men. Hence one should do good deeds in the beginning of day and night. God willing, you will be forgiven during the other times also if Allah wills, because Allah, the Mighty and   Sublime   says:   ‘Therefore   remember   Me,   I   will remember   you’. And   He   says:   ‘and   certainly   the remembrance of Allah is the greatest’.”

records.chapter: Reward of weeping in the fear of Allah
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.translator: Sayed Athar Husain Rizvi & Sayed Maqsood Athar
records.englishText: 1. My father (rah) said: it was narrated to me by Abdillah b. Ja’far from Ibrahim b. Mahziyar from his brother Ali b. Mahziyar from Ibn Abi ’Umayr from Mansur b. Yunis from Muhammad b. Marwan from Abi Abdillah (a.s.) who said: “There is nothing but that it has a measure and a weight except the tears. For indeed a drop of it can extinguish a sea of fire. And when ones eyes are filled with tears, his face will never see poverty or disgrace. And when tears are flowing, Allah prohibits the fire of Hell on it and even if one person is weeping the whole community is dealt with mercy (by Allah).”
records.arabicText: أَبِی ره قَالَ حَدَّثَنِی عَبْدُ اللَّهِ بْنُ جَعْفَرٍ عَنْ إِبْرَاهِیمَ بْنِ مَهْزِیَارَ عَنْ أَخِیهِ عَلِیِّ بْنِ مَهْزِیَارَ عَنِ ابْنِ أَبِی عُمَیْرٍ عَنْ مَنْصُورِ بْنِ یُونُسَ عَنْ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ مَرْوَانَ عَنْ أَبِی عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ع قَالَ: مَا مِنْ شَیْ ءٍ إِلَّا وَ لَهُ کَیْلٌ وَ وَزْنٌ إِلَّا الدُّمُوعَ فَإِنَّ الْقَطْرَهَ مِنْهَا تُطْفِئُ بِحَاراً مِنْ نَارٍ وَ إِذَا اغْرَوْرَقَتِ الْعَیْنُ بِمَائِهَا لَمْ یَرْهَقْ وَجْهَهُ قَتَرٌ وَ لا ذِلَّهٌ فَإِذَا فَاضَتْ حَرَّمَهُ اللَّهُ عَلَی النَّارِ وَ لَوْ أَنَّ بَاکِیاً بَکَی فِی أُمَّهٍ لَرُحِمُوا.
records.thaqalaynSanad: 1. My father (rah) said: it was narrated to me by Abdillah b. Ja’far from Ibrahim b. Mahziyar from his brother Ali b. Mahziyar from Ibn Abi ’Umayr from Mansur b. Yunis from Muhammad b. Marwan from Abi Abdillah (a.s.) who said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: “There is nothing but that it has a measure and a weight except the tears. For indeed a drop of it can extinguish a sea of fire. And when ones eyes are filled with tears, his face will never see poverty or disgrace. And when tears are flowing, Allah prohibits the fire of Hell on it and even if one person is weeping the whole community is dealt with mercy (by Allah).”

records.chapter: Reward of weeping in the fear of Allah
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.translator: Sayed Athar Husain Rizvi & Sayed Maqsood Athar
records.englishText: 2.  Imam Ja’far Sadiq (a.s.) narrates from his predecessors that the Holy Prophet (s.a.w.s.) said, “Tooba (congratulations) for that face which Allah sees mercifully and that person who is weeping  over  his  sins  in  fear  of  Allah,  the  Mighty  and Sublime and none else is aware of this sin.”
records.arabicText: حَدَّثَنِی الْحُسَیْنُ بْنُ أَحْمَدَ عَنْ أَبِیهِ عَنْ عُبَیْدِ اللَّهِ بْنِ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ عِیسَی عَنْ أَبِیهِ عَنْ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ [بْنِ] الْمُغِیرَهِ عَنْ إِسْمَاعِیلَ بْنِ أَبِی زِیَادٍ عَنِ الصَّادِقِ جَعْفَرِ بْنِ مُحَمَّدٍ عَنْ أَبِیهِ ع قَالَ قَالَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ص طُوبَی لِصُورَهٍ نَظَرَ اللَّهُ إِلَیْهَا تَبْکِی عَلَی ذَنْبٍ مِنْ خَشْیَهِ اللَّهِ عَزَّ وَ جَلَّ لَمْ یَطَّلِعْ إِلَی ذَلِکَ الذَّنْبِ غَیْرُهُ.
records.thaqalaynSanad: 2.  Imam Ja’far Sadiq (a.s.) narrates from his predecessors that the Holy Prophet (s.a.w.s.) said,
records.thaqalaynMatn: “Tooba (congratulations) for that face which Allah sees mercifully and that person who is weeping  over  his  sins  in  fear  of  Allah,  the  Mighty  and Sublime and none else is aware of this sin.”

records.chapter: Reward of giving preference to the pleasure of Allah over one’s desires
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.translator: Sayed Athar Husain Rizvi & Sayed Maqsood Athar
records.englishText: 1.  It  is  narrated that Imam  Zainul Abideen (a.s.)  said that Allah,  the  Mighty  and  Sublime  says,  “(I  swear)  by  My Honor, Greatness, Beauty, Strength and high-rank, one who gives preference to My pleasure over his desires I will fulfill the aim of his hereafter. I will make his heart self-sufficient, earth and heavens are guarantors of his sustenance and he will get worldly wealth even without desiring it.”
records.arabicText: حَدَّثَنِی أَحْمَدُ بْنُ مُحَمَّدٍ عَنْ أَبِیهِ عَنِ الْحَسَنِ بْنِ إِسْحَاقَ عَنْ عَلِیِّ بْنِ مَهْزِیَارَ عَنْ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ أَبِی عُمَیْرٍ عَنْ مَنْصُورِ بْنِ یُونُسَ عَنْ أَبِی حَمْزَهَ الثُّمَالِیِّ عَنْ زَیْنِ الْعَابِدِینَ ع یَقُولُ إِنَّ اللَّهَ عَزَّ وَ جَلَّ یَقُولُ وَ عِزَّتِی وَ عَظَمَتِی وَ جَلَالِی وَ بَهَائِی وَ عُلُوِّی وَ ارْتِفَاعِ مَکَانِی لَا یُؤْثِرُ عَبْدٌ هَوَایَ عَلَی هَوَاهُ إِلَّا جَعَلْتُ هَمَّهُ فِی آخِرَتِهِ وَ غِنَاهُ فِی قَلْبِهِ وَ کَفَفْتُ عَلَیْهِ صَنِیعَتَهُ وَ ضَمَّنْتُ السَّمَاوَاتِ وَ الْأَرْضَ رِزْقَهُ وَ آتیه [أَتَتْهُ] الدُّنْیَا وَ هِیَ رَاغِمَهٌ.

records.chapter: Reward of a person whose aim is Hereafter every morning and evening
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.translator: Sayed Athar Husain Rizvi & Sayed Maqsood Athar
records.englishText: 1.  Imam  Ja’far  Sadiq  (a.s.)  narrates  that  the  Holy  Prophet (s.a.w.s.) said, “One who has hereafter as his ultimate aim every  morning  and  evening,  Allah  makes  his  heart  self- sufficient and he will be granted worldly things. He will not pass away from this world without earning his sustenance completely. One who has this world as his aim, Allah will put hunger before his eyes and scatter his worldly affairs and he will not get anything  from the world except what was ordained for him.”
records.arabicText: أَبِی ره قَالَ حَدَّثَنِی سَعْدُ بْنُ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ عَنْ أَحْمَدَ بْنِ مُحَمَّدٍ عَنِ الْحَسَنِ بْنِ مَحْبُوبٍ عَنْ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ بْنِ سِنَانٍ وَ عَبْدِ الْعَزِیزِ بْنِ أَبِی یَعْفُورٍ عَنْ أَبِی عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ع قَالَ قَالَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ص مَنْ أَصْبَحَ وَ أَمْسَی وَ الْآخِرَهُ أَکْبَرُ هَمِّهِ جَعَلَ اللَّهُ لَهُ الْقَنَاعَهَ فِی قَلْبِهِ وَ جَمَعَ لَهُ أَمْرَهُ وَ لَمْ یَخْرُجْ مِنَ الدُّنْیَا حَتَّی یَسْتَکْمِلَ رِزْقَهُ وَ مَنْ أَصْبَحَ وَ أَمْسَی وَ الدُّنْیَا أَکْبَرُ هَمِّهِ جَعَلَ اللَّهُ الْفَقْرَ بَیْنَ عَیْنَیْهِ وَ شَتَّتَ عَلَیْهِ أَمْرَهُ وَ لَمْ یَنَلْ مِنَ الدُّنْیَا إِلَّا مَا قُسِّمَ لَهُ.
records.thaqalaynSanad: 1.  Imam  Ja’far  Sadiq  (a.s.)  narrates  that  the  Holy  Prophet (s.a.w.s.) said,
records.thaqalaynMatn: “One who has hereafter as his ultimate aim every  morning  and  evening,  Allah  makes  his  heart  self- sufficient and he will be granted worldly things. He will not pass away from this world without earning his sustenance completely. One who has this world as his aim, Allah will put hunger before his eyes and scatter his worldly affairs and he will not get anything  from the world except what was ordained for him.”

records.chapter: Reward of doing good
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.translator: Sayed Athar Husain Rizvi & Sayed Maqsood Athar
records.englishText: 1. It was narrated to me by Muhammad b. al-Hassan who said it was narrated to me by Muhammad b. al-Hassan al-Saffar from Ahmad b. Muhammad from al-Hassan b. Mahbub who said it was narrated to me by Abu Muhammad al-Wabishi from Abi Abdillah (a.s.) who said: “If a believer does good Allah multiplies each of his deed seven hundred times and it is in accordance with the saying of Allah: ‘and Allah multiplies for whom He pleases’.”18
records.arabicText: حَدَّثَنِی مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ الْحَسَنِ قَالَ حَدَّثَنِی مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ الْحَسَنِ الصَّفَّارُ عَنْ أَحْمَدَ بْنِ مُحَمَّدٍ عَنِ الْحَسَنِ بْنِ مَحْبُوبٍ قَالَ حَدَّثَنِی أَبُو مُحَمَّدٍ الْوَابِشِیُّ عَنْ أَبِی عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ع قَالَ: إِذَا أَحْسَنَ الْعَبْدُ الْمُؤْمِنُ ضَاعَفَ اللَّهُ لَهُ عَمَلَهُ بِکُلِّ حَسَنَهٍ سَبْعَمِائَهِ ضِعْفٍ وَ ذَلِکَ قَوْلُ اللَّهِ عَزَّ وَ جَلَ وَ اللَّهُ یُضاعِفُ لِمَنْ یَشاءُ
records.thaqalaynSanad: 1. It was narrated to me by Muhammad b. al-Hassan who said it was narrated to me by Muhammad b. al-Hassan al-Saffar from Ahmad b. Muhammad from al-Hassan b. Mahbub who said it was narrated to me by Abu Muhammad al-Wabishi from Abi Abdillah (a.s.) who said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: “If a believer does good Allah multiplies each of his deed seven hundred times and it is in accordance with the saying of Allah: ‘and Allah multiplies for whom He pleases’.”18

records.chapter: Reward of loving, hating, granting and depriving for the sake of Allah
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.translator: Sayed Athar Husain Rizvi & Sayed Maqsood Athar
records.englishText: 1. My father (rah) said: it was narrated to me by Sa’d b. Abdillah from Ahmad b. Muhammad from al-Hassan b. Mahbub from Malik b. ’Atiyyah from Sa’eed al-A’raj from Abi Abdillah (a.s.) who said: “One of the most definite signs of belief is that one loves for the sake of Allah, hates for the sake of Allah, grants for the sake of Allah and deprives for the sake of Allah.”
records.arabicText: أَبِی ره قَالَ حَدَّثَنِی سَعْدُ بْنُ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ عَنْ أَحْمَدَ بْنِ مُحَمَّدٍ عَنِ الْحَسَنِ بْنِ مَحْبُوبٍ عَنْ مَالِکِ بْنِ عَطِیَّهَ عَنْ سَعِیدٍ الْأَعْرَجِ عَنْ أَبِی عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ع قَالَ: مِنْ أَوْثَقِ عُرَی الْإِیمَانِ أَنْ یُحِبَّ فِی اللَّهِ وَ یُبْغِضَ فِی اللَّهِ وَ یُعْطِیَ فِی اللَّهِ وَ یَمْنَعَ فِی اللَّهِ.
records.thaqalaynSanad: 1. My father (rah) said: it was narrated to me by Sa’d b. Abdillah from Ahmad b. Muhammad from al-Hassan b. Mahbub from Malik b. ’Atiyyah from Sa’eed al-A’raj from Abi Abdillah (a.s.) who said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: “One of the most definite signs of belief is that one loves for the sake of Allah, hates for the sake of Allah, grants for the sake of Allah and deprives for the sake of Allah.”

records.chapter: Reward of being ashamed and repenting for one’s sins
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.translator: Sayed Athar Husain Rizvi & Sayed Maqsood Athar
records.englishText: 1. It was narrated to me by Muhammad b. Musa b. al-Mutawakil who said it was narrated to me by Abdillah b. Ja’far al-Himyari from Ahmad b. Muhammad from al-Hassan b. Mahbub from Hisham b. Salim from some of the companions Abi Abdillah (a.s.) who said: Astaghfirullaahalladhee laa ilaaha illaa huwal h’ayyul qayyoomu badeeu’s samaawaati wal arz”i dhaljalaali wal ikraami wa as-aluhu any-yatooba a’lay and Allah does not forgive his sins; and there is no good in one who commits more than forty greater sins.”
records.arabicText: حَدَّثَنِی مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ مُوسَی بْنِ الْمُتَوَکِّلِ قَالَ حَدَّثَنِی عَبْدُ اللَّهِ بْنُ جَعْفَرٍ الْحِمْیَرِیُّ عَنْ أَحْمَدَ بْنِ مُحَمَّدٍ عَنِ الْحَسَنِ بْنِ مَحْبُوبٍ عَنْ هِشَامِ بْنِ سَالِمٍ عَنْ بَعْضِ أَصْحَابِ أَبِی عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ع قَالَ: مَا مِنْ مُؤْمِنٍ یُقَارِفُ فِی یَوْمِهِ وَ لَیْلَتِهِ أَرْبَعِینَ کَبِیرَهً فَیَقُولُ وَ هُوَ نَادِمٌ أَسْتَغْفِرُ اللَّهَ الَّذِی لا إِلهَ إِلَّا هُوَ الْحَیُّ الْقَیُّومُ- بَدِیعُ السَّماواتِ وَ الْأَرْضِ ذا [ذُو] الْجَلالِ وَ الْإِکْرامِ* وَ أَسْأَلُهُ أَنْ یُصَلِّیَ عَلَی مُحَمَّدٍ وَ آلِ مُحَمَّدٍ وَ أَنْ یَتُوبَ عَلَیَّ إِلَّا غَفَرَهَا وَ لَا خَیْرَ فِیمَنْ یُقَارِفُ فِی کُلِّ یَوْمٍ أَکْثَرَ مِنْ أَرْبَعِینَ کَبِیرَهً.
records.thaqalaynSanad: 1. It was narrated to me by Muhammad b. Musa b. al-Mutawakil who said it was narrated to me by Abdillah b. Ja’far al-Himyari from Ahmad b. Muhammad from al-Hassan b. Mahbub from Hisham b. Salim from some of the companions Abi Abdillah (a.s.) who said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: Astaghfirullaahalladhee laa ilaaha illaa huwal h’ayyul qayyoomu badeeu’s samaawaati wal arz”i dhaljalaali wal ikraami wa as-aluhu any-yatooba a’lay and Allah does not forgive his sins; and there is no good in one who commits more than forty greater sins.”

records.chapter: Reward of death of a believer who is away from his home
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.translator: Sayed Athar Husain Rizvi & Sayed Maqsood Athar
records.englishText: 1. It was narrated to me by Muhammad b. Ali Majluwayh from his uncle Muhammad b. Abi al-Qasim from Ahmad b. Abi Abdillah from al-Hassan b. Mahbub from Muhammad b. Marid from Abi Abdillah (a.s.) who said: “No believer passes away while he is away from his home having no one to weep over him except that the parts of the earth where he used to worship and the clothes, which he used to wear will weep for him. All those doors of heaven weep for him, from which his deeds used to go up and the angels appointed on him will weep for him.”
records.arabicText: حَدَّثَنِی مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ عَلِیٍّ مَاجِیلَوَیْهِ عَنْ عَمِّهِ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ أَبِی الْقَاسِمِ عَنْ أَحْمَدَ بْنِ أَبِی عَبْدِ اللَّهِ عَنِ الْحَسَنِ بْنِ مَحْبُوبٍ عَنْ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ مَارِدٍ عَنْ أَبِی عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ع قَالَ: مَا مِنْ مُؤْمِنٍ یَمُوتُ فِی غُرْبَهٍ مِنَ الْأَرْضِ تَغِیبُ فِیهَا بَوَاکِیهِ إِلَّا بَکَتْهُ بِقَاعُ الْأَرَضِینَ الَّذِی کَانَ یَتَعَبَّدُ اللَّهَ فِیهَا وَ بَکَتْهُ أَبْوَابُهُ وَ بَکَتْهُ أَبْوَابُ السَّمَاءِ الَّتِی کَانَ یَصْعَدُ فِیهَا عَمَلُهُ وَ بَکَاهُ الْمَلَکَانِ الْمُوَکَّلَانِ بِهِ.
records.thaqalaynSanad: 1. It was narrated to me by Muhammad b. Ali Majluwayh from his uncle Muhammad b. Abi al-Qasim from Ahmad b. Abi Abdillah from al-Hassan b. Mahbub from Muhammad b. Marid from Abi Abdillah (a.s.) who said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: “No believer passes away while he is away from his home having no one to weep over him except that the parts of the earth where he used to worship and the clothes, which he used to wear will weep for him. All those doors of heaven weep for him, from which his deeds used to go up and the angels appointed on him will weep for him.”

records.chapter: Reward of a disbeliever who does good to a believer
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.translator: Sayed Athar Husain Rizvi & Sayed Maqsood Athar
records.englishText: 1.  Imam Musa Kadhim (a.s.) said, “A believer of Bani Israel had a neighbor who was an infidel and he used to behave very well with the believer and performed good deeds in the world. When the infidel died, Allah built for him a house of mud in Hell which protected him from its heat and provided him  sustenance  from  elsewhere.  He  was  told,  ‘You  are getting these comforts because of your nice behavior with your so and so believing neighbor and doing good in the world’.”
records.arabicText: أَبِی ره قَالَ حَدَّثَنِی سَعْدُ بْنُ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ قَالَ حَدَّثَنِی الْهَیْثَمُ بْنُ أَبِی مَسْرُوقٍ النَّهْدِیُّ عَنِ الْحَسَنِ بْنِ مَحْبُوبٍ عَنْ عَلِیِّ بْنِ یَقْطِینٍ قَالَ قَالَ أَبُو الْحَسَنِ مُوسَی بْنُ جَعْفَرٍ ع إِنَّهُ کَانَ فِی بَنِی إِسْرَائِیلَ رَجُلٌ مُؤْمِنٌ وَ کَانَ لَهُ جَارٌ کَافِرٌ وَ کَانَ یَرْفُقُ بِالْمُؤْمِنِ وَ یُوَلِّیهِ الْمَعْرُوفَ فِی الدُّنْیَا فَلَمَّا أَنْ مَاتَ الْکَافِرُ بَنَی اللَّهُ لَهُ بَیْتاً فِی النَّارِ مِنْ طِینٍ فَکَانَ یَقِیهِ حَرَّهَا وَ یَأْتِیهِ الرِّزْقُ مِنْ غَیْرِهَا وَ قِیلَ لَهُ هَذَا بِمَا کُنْتَ تَدْخُلُ عَلَی جَارِکَ الْمُؤْمِنِ فُلَانِ بْنِ فُلَانٍ مِنَ الرِّفْقِ وَ تُوَلِّیهِ مِنَ الْمَعْرُوفِ فِی الدُّنْیَا.

records.chapter: Reward of doing good to a believing brother
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.translator: Sayed Athar Husain Rizvi & Sayed Maqsood Athar
records.englishText: 1.  Imam  Ja’far  Sadiq  (a.s.),  “One  who  does  good  to  his believing  brother  is  like  one  who  does  good  to  the Messenger of Allah (s.a.w.s.).”
records.arabicText: حَدَّثَنِی مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ الْحَسَنِ قَالَ حَدَّثَنِی مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ الْحَسَنِ الصَّفَّارُ عَنْ أَحْمَدَ بْنِ مُحَمَّدٍ عَنِ الْحَسَنِ بْنِ مَحْبُوبٍ عَنْ جَمِیلٍ عَنْ حَدِیدٍ أَوْ مُرَازِمٍ قَالَ قَالَ أَبُو عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ع أَیُّمَا مُؤْمِنٍ أَوْصَلَ إِلَی أَخِیهِ الْمُؤْمِنِ مَعْرُوفاً فَقَدْ أَوْصَلَ ذَلِکَ إِلَی رَسُولِ اللَّهِ ص.

records.chapter: Reward of rearing goat/sheep for milk
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.translator: Sayed Athar Husain Rizvi & Sayed Maqsood Athar
records.englishText: 1.  It  is  narrated  that  Imam  Ja’far  Sadiq  (a.s.)  said,  “No believer  has  a  milching  animal  in  his  home  but  that  the people of that house will be made holy and they would be congratulated. If he has two goats they will be made holy and given glad-tidings twice everyday.” Some companions asked,  “How  will  he  be  made  holy?”  The  Holy  Prophet (s.a.w.s.) answered, “An angel will stand near  them  every morning and evening and say: ‘You have become holy and are  congratulated.  You  have  become  pure  and  your  food too.” I asked, “What does ‘You have become holy’ means?” He replied, “You have become pure.”
records.arabicText: حَدَّثَنِی مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ عَلِیٍّ مَاجِیلَوَیْهِ عَنْ عَمِّهِ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ أَبِی الْقَاسِمِ عَنْ أَحْمَدَ بْنِ أَبِی عَبْدِ اللَّهِ عَنِ الْحَسَنِ بْنِ مَحْبُوبٍ عَنْ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ مَارِدٍ قَالَ سَمِعْتُ أَبَا عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ع یَقُولُ مَا مِنْ مُؤْمِنٍ یَکُونُ فِی مَنْزِلِهِ عَنْزٌ حَلُوبٌ إِلَّا قُدِّسَ أَهْلُ ذَلِکَ الْمَنْزِلِ وَ بُورِکَ عَلَیْهِمْ وَ إِنْ کَانَتِ اثْنَتَیْنِ قُدِّسُوا وَ بُورِکَ عَلَیْهِمْ کُلَّ یَوْمٍ مَرَّتَیْنِ وَ قَالَ بَعْضُ أَصْحَابِنَا وَ کَیْفَ یُقَدَّسُونَ قَالَ یَقِفُ عَلَیْهِمْ مَلَکٌ کُلَّ صَبَاحٍ وَ مَسَاءٍ فَیَقُولُ قُدِّسْتُمْ وَ بُورِکَ عَلَیْکُمْ وَ طِبْتُمْ وَ طَابَ إِدَامُکُمْ فَقُلْتُ لَهُ مَا مَعْنَی قُدِّسْتُمْ قَالَ طُهِّرْتُمْ.

records.chapter: Reward of Salaat, Zakat, doing good and Patience
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.translator: Sayed Athar Husain Rizvi & Sayed Maqsood Athar
records.englishText: 1. My father (rah) said: it was narrated to me by Sa’d b. Abdillah from Ahmad b. Muhammad from al-Hassan b. Mahbub from Abdillah b. Marhum from Ibn Sinan from Abi Abdillah (a.s.) who said: “When a believer enters his grave, the Salaat is to his right and Zakat to his left. Good deeds shade him and patience stands at one end. When the two angels enter to question him, Patience tells Salaat, Zakat and good deeds: ‘Protect your friend. If you fail to do so, I will protect him.”
records.arabicText: أَبِی ره قَالَ حَدَّثَنِی سَعْدُ بْنُ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ عَنْ أَحْمَدَ بْنِ مُحَمَّدٍ عَنِ الْحَسَنِ بْنِ مَحْبُوبٍ عَنْ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ بْنِ مَرْحُومٍ عَنِ ابْنِ سِنَانٍ عَنْ أَبِی عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ع قَالَ: إِذَا دَخَلَ الْمُؤْمِنُ من قَبْرَهُ کَانَتِ الصَّلَاهُ عَنْ یَمِینِهِ وَ الزَّکَاهُ عَنْ یَسَارِهِ وَ الْبِرُّ مُظِلٌّ عَلَیْهِ وَ ینتحی [یَتَنَحَّی] الصَّبْرُ نَاحِیَهً قَالَ فَإِذَا دَخَلَ عَلَیْهِ الْمَلَکَانِ اللَّذَانِ یَلِیَانِ مُسَاءَلَتَهُ قَالَ الصَّبْرُ لِلصَّلَاهِ وَ الزَّکَاهِ وَ الْبِرِّ دُونَکُمْ صَاحِبَکُمْ فَإِنْ عَجَزْتُمْ عَنْهُ فَإِنَّا دُونَهُ.
records.thaqalaynSanad: 1. My father (rah) said: it was narrated to me by Sa’d b. Abdillah from Ahmad b. Muhammad from al-Hassan b. Mahbub from Abdillah b. Marhum from Ibn Sinan from Abi Abdillah (a.s.) who said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: “When a believer enters his grave, the Salaat is to his right and Zakat to his left. Good deeds shade him and patience stands at one end. When the two angels enter to question him, Patience tells Salaat, Zakat and good deeds: ‘Protect your friend. If you fail to do so, I will protect him.”

records.chapter: Reward of loving the progeny of Muhammad (s.a.w.s.) and hating their enemies for the sake of Allah
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.translator: Sayed Athar Husain Rizvi & Sayed Maqsood Athar
records.englishText: 1. My father (rah) said: it was narrated to me by Muhammad b. Yahya from Ahmad b. Muhammad from al-Hassan b. Mahbub from Salih b. Sahl al-Madaini from Abi Abdillah (a.s.) who said: “If one loves us and hates our enemies for the sake of Allah and dies even after having committed sins equal to the drops of water of the sea then too Allah will forgive him.”
records.arabicText: أَبِی ره قَالَ حَدَّثَنِی مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ یَحْیَی عَنْ أَحْمَدَ بْنِ مُحَمَّدٍ عَنِ الْحَسَنِ بْنِ مَحْبُوبٍ عَنْ صَالِحِ بْنِ سَهْلٍ الْمَدَائِنِیِّ عَنْ أَبِی عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ع قَالَ: مَنْ أَحَبَّنَا وَ أَبْغَضَ عَدُوَّنَا فِی اللَّهِ مِنْ غَیْرِ وَتِیرَهٍ وَتَرَهَا إِیَّاهُ لِشَیْ ءٍ مِنْ أَمْرِ الدُّنْیَا ثُمَّ مَاتَ عَلَی ذَلِکَ وَ عَلَیْهِ مِنَ الذُّنُوبِ مِثْلُ زَبَدِ الْبَحْرِ غَفَرَهَا اللَّهُ لَهُ.
records.thaqalaynSanad: 1. My father (rah) said: it was narrated to me by Muhammad b. Yahya from Ahmad b. Muhammad from al-Hassan b. Mahbub from Salih b. Sahl al-Madaini from Abi Abdillah (a.s.) who said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: “If one loves us and hates our enemies for the sake of Allah and dies even after having committed sins equal to the drops of water of the sea then too Allah will forgive him.”

records.chapter: Reward of reciting Astaghfirullaah seventy times in Salaat-e-Witr for one year
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.translator: Sayed Athar Husain Rizvi & Sayed Maqsood Athar
records.englishText: 1. It was narrated to me by Muhammad b. Muhammad from his father from Ahmad b. Muhammad b. Isa from al-Hassan b. Mahbub from Umar b. Yazid and I do not know it except from Abi Abdillah (a.s.) who said: “One who recites Astaghfirullaaha wa atoobu ilaih seventy times in Salaat-e- Witr for one year the Almighty Allah will write him among those who repent at dawn and it will become obligatory on Allah, the Mighty and Sublime to forgive him.”
records.arabicText: حَدَّثَنِی مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ مُحَمَّدٍ عَنْ أَبِیهِ عَنْ أَحْمَدَ بْنِ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ عِیسَی عَنِ الْحَسَنِ بْنِ مَحْبُوبٍ عَنْ عُمَرَ بْنِ یَزِیدَ وَ لَا أَعْلَمُهُ إِلَّا عَنْ أَبِی عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ع قَالَ: مَنْ قَالَ فِی وَتْرِهِ إِذَا أَوْتَرَ- أَسْتَغْفِرُ اللَّهَ وَ أَتُوبُ إِلَیْهِ سَبْعِینَ مَرَّهً وَ هُوَ قَائِمٌ فَوَاظَبَ عَلَی ذَلِکَ حَتَّی مَضَی لَهُ سَنَهٌ کَتَبَهُ اللَّهُ عِنْدَهُ مِنَ الْمُسْتَغْفِرِینَ بِالْأَسْحارِ وَ وَجَبَتْ لَهُ الْمَغْفِرَهُ مِنَ اللَّهِ عَزَّ وَ جَلَّ.
records.thaqalaynSanad: 1. It was narrated to me by Muhammad b. Muhammad from his father from Ahmad b. Muhammad b. Isa from al-Hassan b. Mahbub from Umar b. Yazid and I do not know it except from Abi Abdillah (a.s.) who said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: “One who recites Astaghfirullaaha wa atoobu ilaih seventy times in Salaat-e- Witr for one year the Almighty Allah will write him among those who repent at dawn and it will become obligatory on Allah, the Mighty and Sublime to forgive him.”

records.chapter: Reward of saluting a believing brother for the sake of Allah
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.translator: Sayed Athar Husain Rizvi & Sayed Maqsood Athar
records.englishText: 1. My father (rah) said: it was narrated to me by Sa’d b. Abdillah from Ahmad b. Muhammad from al-Hassan b. Mahbub from Abi Jameela from Abi Ja’far al-Baqir (a.s.) who said: "that an angel passed by a person standing near a door. The angel asked him, “O servant of Allah! Why are you standing here?” He replied, “One of my believing brothers lives here. I want to salute him.” The angel asked, “Is he your near relative or you are having some work with him?” He replied, “He is neither a near relative of mine nor do I have any work with him. He is just a Muslim brother for me and hence, I have come to pay respect to him. I have made it obligatory on myself to salute him for the sake of pleasure of the Lord of the worlds.” The angel said, “Allah has sent me to you to convey His salutations and has said: Your only aim was to please Me and you met Me. I have made Paradise obligatory for you, given you salvation from My anger so that you are granted freedom from the fire of Hell."
records.arabicText: أَبِی ره قَالَ حَدَّثَنِی سَعْدُ بْنُ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ عَنْ أَحْمَدَ بْنِ مُحَمَّدٍ عَنِ الْحَسَنِ بْنِ مَحْبُوبٍ عَنْ أَبِی جَمِیلَهَ عَنْ أَبِی جَعْفَرٍ الْبَاقِرِ ع قَالَ: إِنَّ مَلَکاً مِنَ الْمَلَائِکَهِ مَرَّ بِرَجُلٍ قَائِمٍ عَلَی بَابِ دَارٍ فَقَالَ لَهُ الْمَلَکُ یَا عَبْدَ اللَّهِ مَا وُقُوفُکَ عَلَی بَابِ هَذِهِ الدَّارِ قَالَ فَقَالَ لَهُ أَخٌ لِی فِیهَا أَرَدْتُ أَنْ أُسَلِّمَ عَلَیْهِ فَقَالَ لَهُ الْمَلَکُ هَلْ بَیْنَکَ وَ بَیْنَهُ رَحِمٌ مَاسَّهٌ أَوْ هَلْ تُرَغِّبُکَ إِلَیْهِ حَاجَهٌ قَالَ فَقَالَ لَا [مَا] بَیْنِی وَ بَیْنَهُ قَرَابَهٌ وَ لَا یُرَغِّبُنِی إِلَیْهِ حَاجَهٌ إِلَّا أُخُوَّهُ الْإِسْلَامِ وَ حُرْمَتُهُ فَإِنَّمَا أَتَعَهَّدُهُ أُسَلِّمُ عَلَیْهِ فِی اللَّهِ رَبِّ الْعَالَمِینَ فَقَالَ لَهُ الْمَلَکُ إِنِّی رَسُولُ اللَّهِ إِلَیْکَ وَ هُوَ یُقْرِئُکَ السَّلَامَ وَ یَقُولُ إِنَّمَا إِیَّایَ أَرَدْتَ وَ تَعَاهَدْتَ وَ قَدْ أَوْجَبْتُ لَکَ الْجَنَّهَ وَ أَعْفَیْتُکَ مِنْ غَضَبِی وَ أَجَرْتُکَ مِنَ النَّارِ.
records.thaqalaynSanad: 1. My father (rah) said: it was narrated to me by Sa’d b. Abdillah from Ahmad b. Muhammad from al-Hassan b. Mahbub from Abi Jameela from Abi Ja’far al-Baqir (a.s.) who said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "that an angel passed by a person standing near a door. The angel asked him, “O servant of Allah! Why are you standing here?” He replied, “One of my believing brothers lives here. I want to salute him.” The angel asked, “Is he your near relative or you are having some work with him?” He replied, “He is neither a near relative of mine nor do I have any work with him. He is just a Muslim brother for me and hence, I have come to pay respect to him. I have made it obligatory on myself to salute him for the sake of pleasure of the Lord of the worlds.” The angel said, “Allah has sent me to you to convey His salutations and has said: Your only aim was to please Me and you met Me. I have made Paradise obligatory for you, given you salvation from My anger so that you are granted freedom from the fire of Hell."

records.chapter: Reward of doing Taubah Nasooh
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.translator: Sayed Athar Husain Rizvi & Sayed Maqsood Athar
records.englishText: 1. My father (rah) said: it was narrated to me by Ahmad b. Idris from Ahmad b. Muhammad from al-Hassan b. Mahbub from Muawiyah b. Wahab who said: “When a person does Taubah Nasooh, Allah loves him; and his sins of this world and hereafter are concealed.” The narrator asked, “How does Allah conceal all his sins?” Imam (a.s.) replied, “He makes the two angels who write deeds forget those sins and orders the parts of his body to keep the sins hidden. All the parts of the earth where he has committed sins are ordered to hide his sins. Hence when that person comes in the presence of Allah, nothing in this universe will testify to even the smallest of his sins.”
records.arabicText: أَبِی ره قَالَ حَدَّثَنِی أَحْمَدُ بْنُ إِدْرِیسَ عَنْ أَحْمَدَ بْنِ مُحَمَّدٍ عَنِ الْحَسَنِ بْنِ مَحْبُوبٍ عَنْ مُعَاوِیَهَ بْنِ وَهْبٍ قَالَ: سَمِعْتُ أَبَا عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ع یَقُولُ إِذَا تَابَ الْعَبْدُ الْمُؤْمِنُ تَوْبَهً نَصُوحاً أَحَبَّهُ اللَّهُ فَسَتَرَ عَلَیْهِ فِی الدُّنْیَا وَ الْآخِرَهِ قُلْتُ وَ کَیْفَ یَسْتُرُ عَلَیْهِ قَالَ یُنْسِی مَلَکَیْهِ مَا کَتَبَا عَلَیْهِ مِنَ الذُّنُوبِ وَ أَوْحَی اللَّهُ إِلَی جَوَارِحِهِ اکْتُمِی عَلَیْهِ ذُنُوبَهُ وَ أَوْحَی إِلَی بِقَاعِ الْأَرْضِ اکْتُمِی عَلَیْهِ مَا کَانَ یَعْمَلُ عَلَیْکِ مِنَ الذُّنُوبِ فَیَلْقَی اللَّهَ حِینَ یَلْقَاهُ وَ لَیْسَ شَیْ ءٌ یَشْهَدُ عَلَیْهِ بِالذُّنُوبِ.
records.thaqalaynSanad: 1. My father (rah) said: it was narrated to me by Ahmad b. Idris from Ahmad b. Muhammad from al-Hassan b. Mahbub from Muawiyah b. Wahab who said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: “When a person does Taubah Nasooh, Allah loves him; and his sins of this world and hereafter are concealed.” The narrator asked, “How does Allah conceal all his sins?” Imam (a.s.) replied, “He makes the two angels who write deeds forget those sins and orders the parts of his body to keep the sins hidden. All the parts of the earth where he has committed sins are ordered to hide his sins. Hence when that person comes in the presence of Allah, nothing in this universe will testify to even the smallest of his sins.”

records.chapter: Reward of not having expectations, good character, soft behavior and simple nature
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.translator: Sayed Athar Husain Rizvi & Sayed Maqsood Athar
records.englishText: 1. My father (rah) said: it was narrated to me by Sa’d b. Abdillah from Muhammad b. al-Hussain b. Abi al-Khattab from al-Abbas b. Ma’ruf from Sa’d b. Muslim from Abdillah b. Sinan from Abi Abdillah (a.s.) who said: “The Messenger of Allah (sw) said: 'Shall I not inform you about the one whom the Fire will be forbidden for him tomorrow?' They said: 'Yes, oh Messenger of Allah!' He said: 'The one who does not expect, has good character, soft behavior and simple nature.'”
records.arabicText: أَبِی ره قَالَ حَدَّثَنِی سَعْدُ بْنُ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ عَنْ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ الْحُسَیْنِ بْنِ أَبِی الْخَطَّابِ عَنِ الْعَبَّاسِ بْنِ مَعْرُوفٍ عَنْ سَعْدِ بْنِ مُسْلِمٍ عَنْ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ بْنِ سِنَانٍ عَنْ أَبِی عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ع قَالَ: قَالَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ص أَ لَا أُخْبِرُکُمْ بِمَنْ تَحْرُمُ عَلَیْهِ النَّارُ غَداً قَالُوا بَلَی یَا رَسُولَ اللَّهِ قَالَ الْهَیِّنُ الْقَرِیبُ اللَّیِّنُ السَّهْلُ.
records.thaqalaynSanad: 1. My father (rah) said: it was narrated to me by Sa’d b. Abdillah from Muhammad b. al-Hussain b. Abi al-Khattab from al-Abbas b. Ma’ruf from Sa’d b. Muslim from Abdillah b. Sinan from Abi Abdillah (a.s.) who said: “
records.thaqalaynMatn: The Messenger of Allah (sw) said: 'Shall I not inform you about the one whom the Fire will be forbidden for him tomorrow?' They said: 'Yes, oh Messenger of Allah!' He said: 'The one who does not expect, has good character, soft behavior and simple nature.'”
